heroHeadline: Min Aung Hlaing Elected VP (247/260 Votes) as AA Siege Tightens Around Sittwe
heroSubtitle: On April 1, Myanmar's military-packed parliament elected junta chief Min Aung Hlaing as vice-president with 247 of 260 votes — the final step before a predetermined presidential vote. Min Aung Hlaing had formally retired as military Commander-in-Chief on March 30, installing loyalist General Ye Win Oo in his place, to meet the 2008 constitution's civilian candidacy requirement. Simultaneously, the Arakan Army maintained its siege of Sittwe, where approximately 48 junta soldiers deserted as AA fighters held positions 2 km from the city center. On March 30, SAC jets killed approximately 10 civilians including pregnant women and infants in Butalin Township, Sagaing Region — continuing the documented pattern of deliberate civilian targeting.
footerNote: Data sourced from AAPP, ACLED, UN OCHA, Irrawaddy, RFA, Myanmar Now, DVB, Al Jazeera, Mizzima, Human Rights Watch, Crisis Group, Myanmar Peace Monitor, and official NUG, KNU, and AA statements.
